sources.title: Guidelines for the Planning and Design of Police Programs and Facilities

small.name: Typical small-town police station
small.notes: Public lobby, patrol/admin, interview, evidence, limited holding

medium.name: Municipal police headquarters
medium.notes: 24/7 operations, dispatch, evidence, secure sally port

space_library.name: Public Lobby / Report Desk
notes: Public-facing intake, report desk, and controlled access beyond lobby.

space_library.name: Admin / Open Office
notes: General administration, community policing staff, records oversight.

space_library.name: Dispatch / Communications
notes: Secure communications core; visual and acoustic separation are critical.

space_library.name: Interview / Victim Room
notes: Private interviews adjacent to public lobby but outside secure back-of-house.

space_library.name: Evidence Intake / Storage
notes: Controlled chain-of-custody storage with restricted access.

space_library.name: Holding / Temporary Detention
notes: Temporary holding only; keep secure separation from public areas.

space_library.name: Secure Sally Port / Booking
notes: Vehicle-secure intake for detainees and evidence; direct link to temporary holding.

space_library.name: Briefing / Training Room
notes: Shift briefing, training, and community emergency coordination.

space_library.name: Records / Report Writing
notes: Records, report writing, and case-processing support with controlled access.

envelope_assembly.glazing: durable punched openings with security glazing where required

plan_archetypes.notes: Public access, staff operations, and detention/sally-port flows should remain distinct.
